headline: Will Digital Realty’s Lisbon and Sofia Expansion Reframe DLR’s AI Infrastructure and Connectivity Narrative?
summary: Digital Realty has recently expanded into Portugal and Bulgaria by acquiring a Lisbon data center with up to 2.4 MW of IT load and two Sofia facilities, strengthening its presence across Southern and Southeastern Europe’s key connectivity hubs. By positioning its new Lisbon site near major subsea cable landings and tying it into corridors linking Europe, Africa and the Americas, Digital Realty is deepening its role in the infrastructure that underpins AI workloads and low-latency global data...

headline: Digital Realty Enters Portugal, Boosts Southern Europe Data Hub
summary: DLR enters Portugal with a Lisbon data center near key subsea cables, expanding its Southern Europe footprint to meet rising cloud, AI and connectivity demand.

headline: Data center boom boosts communications infra companies but supply catching up
summary: Investing.com -- Demand for data centers and internet infrastructure is outpacing supply benefitting U.S. communications infrastructure companies, but the imbalance is likely to ease in the next few years.

headline: Digital Realty Expands Lisbon And Sofia Presence To Capture AI Data Flows
summary: Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) is expanding into Portugal and Bulgaria with data center acquisitions in Lisbon and Sofia. The Lisbon site is intended to serve as a data gateway between Europe, Africa, and the Americas. The Sofia acquisition gives the company a new foothold in Southeast Europe to support digital infrastructure needs. Digital Realty Trust, a global data center and interconnection operator, is expanding further into Europe as data traffic and AI workloads reshape...
